title: Data Loading Pipeline
interaction_flow: Provider → Provider: get_data_static(idx, frame_indices, view_indices, num_input_multi_views) OR get_data_dynamic(...)
Provider → RadymWrapper: get_data(idx, data_fields, frame_indices, view_indices)
RadymWrapper → Radym: _read_data(video_idx, frame_idxs, [view_idx], data_fields) [per view, concatenates]
Radym → VideoReader: get_batch(frame_indices) → rgb tensor [for IMAGE_RGB]
Radym → FileSystem: np.load(pose/*.npz) → c2w tensor [for CAMERA_C2W_TRANSFORM]
Radym → FileSystem: np.load(intrinsics/*.npz) → intrinsics tensor [for CAMERA_INTRINSICS]
Radym → FileSystem: zipfile+OpenEXR frame_idx.exr → depth tensor [for METRIC_DEPTH]
Radym → FileSystem: torch.load(latent/*.pkl) → latent tensor [for LATENT_RGB]
RadymWrapper → Provider: return merged output_dict (views concatenated)
Provider → Provider: _preprocess(file_name, rgbs, c2ws, intrinsics, depths, timesteps, latents, target_index, num_input_multi_views)
Provider → ImageTransform: preprocess_images(rgbs, depths) → cropped/resized rgbs, depths, shift, scale, flip_flag
Provider → Provider: adjust intrinsics with shift/scale
Provider → Provider: compute relative camera poses (c2w_ref, c2w_rel)
Provider → Provider: split rgbs/c2ws/intrinsics into input and target views
Provider → TrainingLoop: return out_dict {images_output, cam_view, intrinsics, c2ws_input, intrinsics_input, time_embeddings, ...}
get_video_dataloader → AugmentDataset: dataset_entry(dataset_name, 'video', is_train, resolution, crop_height, num_video_frames)
AugmentDataset → AugmentDataset: apply augmentations in sequence → aug_instance(data_dict)
key_relationships: dataset_registry maps dataset_name → {cls: RadymWrapper, kwargs, scene_scale, max_gap, min_gap}
get_video_dataloader uses LazyCall pattern to construct DataLoader lazily

class_signatures.Provider._preprocess: _preprocess(file_name, rgbs, c2ws, intrinsics, depths, timesteps, latents=None, target_index=None, num_input_multi_views=None) -> dict
class_signatures.RadymWrapper.get_data: get_data(idx, data_fields, frame_indices=None, view_indices=None, camera_convention='opencv') -> dict
class_signatures.Radym.__init__: __init__(root_path, filter_list_path, num_views, depth_folder, ...) — scans rgb/*.mp4, LRU zip cache
class_signatures.get_video_dataloader: get_video_dataloader(dataset_name, is_train, batch_size=1, num_video_frames=25, resolution='720', crop_height=128, num_workers=8) -> LazyCall(DataLoader)
class_signatures.dataset_entry: dataset_entry(dataset_name, dataset_type, is_train, resolution, crop_height, num_video_frames) -> AugmentDataset

data_fields: DataField.IMAGE_RGB — read from .mp4 via VideoReader.get_batch(), normalized to [0,1], shape [T,C,H,W]
DataField.CAMERA_C2W_TRANSFORM — read from pose/*.npz, indexed by frame, shape [T,4,4]
DataField.METRIC_DEPTH — read from zip+OpenEXR .exr files, shape [T,H,W]
DataField.LATENT_RGB — read from latent/*.pkl via torch.load, shape [T,...]
output_dict_keys: flip_flag — whether horizontal flip was applied
num_input_multi_views — number of input camera views
important_notes: Provider uses dataset_registry to dynamically instantiate RadymWrapper by name (e.g. 'lyra_static', 'lyra_dynamic')
Static vs dynamic scenes diverge in index sampling: static uses random frame windows; dynamic uses temporal gaps (min_gap=45, max_gap=121)
RadymWrapper adapts BaseDataset interface (read/num_frames) to Provider interface (get_data/count_frames/sample_list)
Radym maintains LRU cache (MAX_ZIP_DESCRIPTORS=10) for zip file handles across worker processes
Multi-view support: n_views=-1 is monocular; n_views>0 reads from subdirectory per view index
Provider.__getitem__ retries up to 20 times on exception before randomly re-sampling an index
get_video_dataloader (Cosmos tokenizer) is a separate pipeline using LazyCall/instantiate pattern — does NOT use Provider/Radym
Image transforms: ImageTransform does crop+resize; then Normalize(mean=0.5, std=0.5) for VAE encoder input
Latents: if load_latents=True, precomputed cosmos latents loaded from .pkl instead of raw RGB for input views
Camera poses made relative to first frame: c2ws_new = inv(c2w_ref) @ c2ws; then scaled by scene_scale
